mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate transmits into tighter financial conditions, raising discount rates and reducing equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS increased month-over-month over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, labor market deterioration triggers recession risk repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of instances when the trigger fires during any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in the CPI index and the federal funds rate tighten the real policy stance abruptly, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of contemporaneous monthly increases in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of joint occurrences over a rolling 3-year window, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (t-stat < 2) in a bivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales signals resilient consumer spending, lifting aggregate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when RSAFS increased month-over-month during consumer-led expansions over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals contracting manufacturing output, reducing aggregate earnings expectations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day S&P 500 returns do not occur in more than 50% of months when INDPRO declines month-over-month over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and downstream demand for construction and durable goods, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of months when HOUST declines month-over-month over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and the CPI index signal a wage-price spiral risk that prompts additional monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
regime_hint: tight labor market with high inflation
failure_condition: The joint condition of month-over-month increases in both PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL does not produce negative 20-day returns in greater than 50% of joint occurrences over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5% and subsequently declines month-over-month, the peak in the tightening cycle reduces discount rate uncertainty, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days.
failure_condition: When the trigger fires, positive 60-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances over any 10-year rolling window.
